expect: Predict >50% of L-1100+ claims are n<50 (directional zone per L-850). Teshuvah test: L-850 guidance exists but isn't followed.
actual: 49.7% under n=50 (matches prediction). 56.6% under n=100. Median n=50. Bimodal distribution: 28% are n<10 (observational/single-experiment), 36% are n>500 (corpus-level measurements). The swarm makes two distinct types of claims that need different source-level classification.
diff: Prediction matched within 0.3pp. Key insight: the bimodal distribution means a single n≥100 threshold is wrong — corpus-level claims (n>500) easily pass, individual experiments (n<10) can never pass. Need two-tier: corpus measurements (n≥100 threshold) vs experimental claims (n≥3 replication threshold). This connects to DRM-H21: source hierarchy would make the claim-type distinction structural.

teshuvah_test.behavioral_change: median n dropped from 51 to 50 (flat). pct_under_50 at 49.7% vs L-1244 report of 58%
teshuvah_test.verdict: PARTIAL FAILURE — corpus-level claims follow guidance automatically; experimental claims systematically violate it. The violation is structural: individual experiments can't reach n=100 in a single session.

intervention: Two-tier claim classification: (1) CORPUS claims (n=lesson/session count) — n>=100 threshold applies. (2) EXPERIMENTAL claims (n=replications) — n>=3 independent replications threshold. This matches DRM-H21 source hierarchy: different evidence types need different thresholds.
